creator: cjr@cruwe.de
text:
I seem to be a bit thick on the uptake today, sorry.

The rc.d-script was not included as diff -uN != diff -urN. My
mistake. It is now in the attached shar. I am reasonably certain thet I
did not miss anything now.

One thing I noticed is that I have a question I forgot to ask: Does the
rc.d-script from the files-section need to be included somehow in the
pkg-plist? I looked at the tomcat port, which does not have it, so is
it safe to conclude that is policy?

To the second question, I could not decide between java/glassfish and
www/glassfish. java/jboss and www/tomcat are both application servers,
yet they ended up in different categories. I am fine with either, yet I
had to decide for something, so I flipped a coin. If you think that
www/glassfish is more appropriate for any reason, make it www/glassfish.

Thanks for taking that up, cheers,
-- 
Christopher 
TZ: 	    GMT + 2h

Punctuation matters:
"Let's eat Grandma" or "Let's eat, Grandma" - Punctuation saves lives.
"A panda eats shoots and leaves" or "A panda eats, shoots, and leaves" -
Punctuation teaches proper biology.
